prose:
Most electrophilic Fukui function (f⁺) of phenol

Method provenance
1. Property: Atom‐condensed Fukui functions f⁺ (electrophilic attack), f⁻ (nucleophilic attack), f⁰ (radical attack), and dual descriptor; identification of the most electrophilic/nucleophilic sites.
2. Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (no density fitting) in PySCF.
3. Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates (phenol.xyz); single–point calculations on this fixed geometry for the N, N+1, and N–1 charge states (no re-optimization).
4. Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation).
5. Charge and multiplicity: neutral singlet parent (charge 0, multiplicity 1); cation state charge +1, multiplicity 2; anion state charge −1, multiplicity 2.
6. Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill → PySCF backend (version not reported in JSON).
7. Convergence & caveats: All three SCF calculations converged; integrity status "ok", trustworthy = true. Charge-conservation checks passed (Σf⁺ = Σf⁻ = 1.0000). No warnings were returned by the engine.

Headline result
• The most electrophilic site is atom 3 (C, index 2) with f⁺ = 2.1028319.
• The most nucleophilic site is atom 1 (O, index 0) with f⁻ = 0.16201477.

Live log & files
The run was executed with:
  cli: chemkit fukui --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1 phenol.xyz
A live engine log was written during execution to a file reported by chemkit (path in the JSON’s cli_invocation). The full result, plot, and JSON are saved alongside that log for inspection.

No further warnings were reported.
